title: [FIRRTL] Sometimes `cmem` unexpectedly generates no wmask port

features.name: cmem with mixed sub-field and whole-entry writes
features.description: Code should include a memory array with struct-type elements where one write access targets a sub-field of the memory element and another write access targets the entire memory element, causing the memory instance to be generated without write mask ports
features.snippet: module data_8x3(input [2:0] W0_addr, input W0_en, input W0_clk, input [2:0] W0_data, input [2:0] W0_mask, input [2:0] W1_addr, input W1_en, input W1_clk, input [2:0] W1_data, input [2:0] W1_mask); reg [2:0] Memory[0:7]; always @(posedge W0_clk) begin if (W0_en & W0_mask[0]) Memory[W0_addr][32'h0 +: 1] <= W0_data[0]; if (W0_en & W0_mask[1]) Memory[W0_addr][32'h1 +: 1] <= W0_data[1]; if (W0_en & W0_mask[2]) Memory[W0_addr][32'h2 +: 1] <= W0_data[2]; end endmodule

features.name: cmem sub-field write using infer mport
features.description: Code should include a memory write access pattern where an infer mport accesses only a sub-field of a memory element (e.g., memory[addr].s1) rather than the entire element
features.snippet: byte unsigned mem [8]; always @(posedge clk) begin if (wen) mem[addr].s1 <= wdata; end

features.name: cmem whole-entry write using infer mport
features.description: Code should include a memory write access pattern where an infer mport writes to the entire memory element (all sub-fields at once) rather than individual sub-fields
features.snippet: byte unsigned mem [8]; always @(posedge clk) begin if (wen) mem[addr] <= wdata; end
